findings: Verified identity: the adult-male portrait matches Turdus merula in glossy black plumage, orange-yellow bill, yellow eye-ring, compact thrush form, dark legs, and relatively long tail.
Verified measurements: length 23.5–29 cm and weight 80–125 g are supported by Wikipedia; wingspan 34–38 cm and weight 80–125 g are supported by Animal Diversity Web.

Verified taxonomy and text: Eurasian Blackbird, Turdus merula, and family Turdidae are correct. The habitat, behavior, diet, migration, song-perch, and male/female field-mark statements are consistent with the inspected sources.

The main bird has one head, one beak, two anatomically implied wings with the far wing occluded by the side-on pose, two legs, and one tail. The separate lower wing and head are clearly comparative detail studies, not duplicated anatomy on the main bird.

Minor anatomical issue: the main portrait’s toes and joints are somewhat oversized and crowded compared with the reference photographs, though the legs and feet remain recognizably avian and do not contain a material duplication.

Minor proportional issue: the illustrated bill is slightly longer and more needle-pointed than in most attached reference photographs, but its color and overall form remain diagnostic for an adult male Eurasian Blackbird.
The plate is clean, balanced, legible, and uncropped, with useful wing, head, palette, and scale studies.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Turdus merula (common blackbird) — Animal Diversity Web
